Engineering case studies

Technical write-ups focused on system design, tradeoffs, and measurable production impact.

These are technical case studies on backend, platform, and optimization systems, written to show the architecture, tradeoffs, and production outcomes behind the work.

Featured studies

Flagship technical stories

Featured

Dynamic scheduling engine

Operational scheduling rebuilt for sub-second decisions

Booking and rescheduling requests were forcing a slow, correctness-sensitive scheduling path that degraded user experience and operational throughput.

Designed and optimized a slot-based scheduling engine for high-volume booking, rescheduling, and cancellation workflows.

~30s -> <1s~1,000 req/sec97.5% latency improvement
PythonScheduling servicesDatabase optimizationCaching
Featured

AndGo platform scaling and modernization

From legacy delivery constraints to platform-grade execution

Legacy architecture and weak engineering operating systems were slowing delivery, increasing rework, and limiting the platform’s ability to scale with the business.

Led backend and platform modernization across fleet, scheduling, pricing, and engineering systems, pairing architecture direction with measurable improvements in delivery and business outcomes.

$3M -> $15M MRR~20% faster delivery15% -> 4.97% rework
PythonFlaskFastAPIGraphQL
Featured

Routing optimization engine

Modeling real field constraints across 800+ locations

Naive routing logic could not reflect the real field constraints that determined on-time service, technician utilization, and downstream schedule stability.

Designed routing optimization for mobile technicians across multi-location field operations, using travel time, service duration, and time windows instead of simplistic distance logic.

800+ locations+40% on-time-50% reschedules
Backend servicesOptimization logicTravel-time modelingOperational workflows

Additional work

Adjacent technical exploration

Supporting work that broadens the portfolio into adjacent platform and AI-enabled system design patterns.

Exploration

RAG knowledge retrieval

Practical AI retrieval for internal engineering and operational knowledge

Internal knowledge was fragmented across tools and documents, which made it hard for engineers and operators to retrieve reliable context quickly enough to support day-to-day decisions.

Architected retrieval-backed internal tooling for engineering and operational knowledge access, grounding AI work in trust, usefulness, and maintainability rather than novelty.

RAG systemsAWS BedrockInternal knowledge access
PythonAWS BedrockVector retrievalRAG pipelines
